what_manipulated: liability (legal requirement of sufficient quality), verifiability (ability to verify actual quality provided), reputation (seller identifiability), competition (seller choice availability)
what_measured: trade frequency, provision quality (undertreatment/overtreatment), overcharging, market efficiency
experiment: Bilateral expert-consumer matching over 16 repeated rounds. Consumers decide entry after observing seller prices; sellers provide quality and charge prices. Manipulated: institutional safeguards (liability/verifiability 2x2) and market structure (reputation/competition 2x2), creating 16 conditions. Measure: trade volume, quality efficiency, undertreatment/overcharging rates, and equilibrium pricing patterns.

what_manipulated: job design elements: effort discretion (full vs. limited), wage level, screening for work attitude (past performance signal), labor market competition
what_measured: effort choice, employer profit, employee earnings, job clustering (control vs. trust strategy)
experiment: Employer-employee matching over 15 periods. Employer offers contract specifying wage and effort discretion; employee accepts/rejects, then chooses effort. Treatments: base (no screening), screening (employer sees 3-period effort history), competition (multiple employers per employee). Measure: effort provision patterns, strategic job design clustering, welfare effects of screening and competition.

what_manipulated: fundamental value process (declining vs. constant), cash-to-asset ratio (increasing vs. constant), experimental context (generic stocks vs. depletable gold mine stocks)
what_measured: asset prices, mispricing (RAD), overvaluation (RD), subject comprehension of fundamental value
experiment: Asset market with 10 subjects trading dividend-paying stock over 10 periods. Factorial design varies fundamental value and cash-to-asset ratio (2x2). Control treatments replace framing with depletable-gold-mine context. Measure: price deviations from fundamental value, overvaluation extent, subject questionnaire responses on FV understanding.

what_manipulated: organ allocation policy (standard waiting list vs. priority for registered donors), cost of donation (monetary incentive variations)
what_measured: organ donor registration decision, donation rate, expected payoff from priority
experiment: 31 repeated donation rounds in fixed groups of 12. Each period subjects decide whether to register as organ donor (pay cost for benefit if they later need organ). Manipulated: allocation rule (control/priority/rebate/discount), cost level (low/high). Measure: registration rates under different allocation policies and cost structures, reinforcement learning effects.

what_manipulated: punishment option availability and severity (none, standard 1:3 ratio, strong 1:6 ratio), monitoring (perfect vs. 10% error noise)
what_measured: public good contributions, punishment assignments, net earnings, cooperation patterns
experiment: 50-round public good game in groups of 3. Subjects choose binary contribution (all-or-none 20 tokens). 2x3 factorial design: punishment severity (none/standard/strong) × monitoring quality (perfect/noisy 10%). Second stage: optional costly punishment with damage multiplier. Measure: contribution levels, punishment patterns, net payoffs, cooperation evolution.

what_manipulated: exchange medium (gift exchange via bilateral matching vs. monetary exchange via trading post), budget constraint design (Lagos-Wright environment with money vs. no-money barter)
what_measured: quantity traded, pricing behavior, efficiency of trade, adherence to social norms
experiment: Decentralized and centralized market meetings across 15 periods. Agents randomly bilaterally matched in decentralized phase for specialized-good production/consumption; then centralized Walrasian phase. Treatments vary exchange medium: gift-exchange protocol vs. monetary exchange with trading post. Measure: trading volumes, price patterns, ability to sustain equilibrium cooperation through explicit vs. implicit contracts.
